methods.method_name: Modern Pollen Training Data Set Selection
methods.description: Process for selecting a modern pollen training data set from sites within a 2000-km radius around Lake Ilirney to ensure taxonomic consistency and cover a large environmental gradient.

steps.description: Select modern pollen training data set from sites within a 2000-km radius around Lake Ilirney.
steps.searchable_summary: select modern pollen data from sites within 2000-km radius

steps.description: Harmonize the selected data set taxonomically with the fossil taxa.
steps.searchable_summary: harmonize pollen data set with fossil taxa

methods.description: Extraction of mean July temperature and annual precipitation data for modern pollen sites from WorldClim 2 data.

steps.description: Extract mean July temperature (T July) and annual precipitation (P ann) for all modern pollen sites from WorldClim 2 data.
steps.searchable_summary: extract climate data from WorldClim 2 for pollen sites

methods.method_name: Climate Reconstruction Using Modern Analogue Technique
methods.description: Perform climate reconstructions using the modern analogue technique (MAT) transfer function with the selected modern pollen training data set.

steps.description: Perform climate reconstructions using the MAT transfer function, taking seven analogues in the modern pollen training data set into account.
steps.searchable_summary: apply MAT transfer function for climate reconstruction

methods.description: Perform statistical significance testing for the climate reconstruction using the randomTF-function.

steps.description: Perform statistical significance test for the reconstruction using the randomTF-function in the palaeoSig package.
Test the significance of T July reconstruction with P ann as a conditional variable and vice versa.
steps.searchable_summary: test significance of T July with P ann as conditional variable

methods.description: Estimate the root mean square error of prediction (RMSEP) from cross-validation of the calibration set.

steps.description: Estimate RMSEP from cross-validation of the calibration set.
